behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Two-pass result: Unit is sufficient because the failure boundary is string segmentation logic itself (single parser/segmenter boundary), with no required OS/network/subprocess/LLM dependency. Rejected Integration after re-check: behavior text is only about segmentation output shape, not cross-module handoff. Rejected System: no real shell execution is required by the behavior text ('newline-separated statements' can be provided as plain input text).
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative multi-line command text (including variable assignment lines and a trailing command line) into the segmentation function/object and assert distinct ordered segments are returned instead of a single block.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Two-pass result: Integration is the minimum real-failure boundary because the incident was caused by interaction between segmentation and trigger classification ('classified the whole block as not_a_trigger'). Unit on matcher alone can miss that handoff bug. Integration-brake checks: no required real OS/network/subprocess behavior for this assertion if command text is injected; no AI dependency; no multi-agent chain. Rejected System: behavior text targets recognition from a multi-line block, not actual `gh` execution.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real segmenter + trigger-classifier modules in-process. Provide a multi-line block containing assignments then `gh pr create ...`; assert classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Two-pass result: Integration remains minimum because correct negative classification depends on the same segmentation→classification handoff that failed in production, just with a neighboring command family. Unit-only matcher tests can miss boundary regressions in segment selection. Rejected System: behavior text is about trigger discrimination (`pr create` vs `issue create`) from input text, not real subprocess outcomes.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process integration test with segmenter + classifier using a multi-line block that mirrors the PR case but ends in `gh issue create`; assert result is explicitly non-PR trigger.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Two-pass result: Integration is needed because this is a cross-module behavior (trigger detection output + identifier reconstruction + state persistence). Unit tests of reconstruction alone would miss wiring failures. Integration-brake: no explicit requirement for real network/subprocess in behavior text; local filesystem persistence can be exercised in-process temp dir. Rejected System: text says 'reconstruct and persist' the identifier, not 'obtain via real gh/API call'.
behaviors.test_description: Using temp filesystem storage, run hook flow from detected PR-create event through identifier reconstruction and state write; assert persisted state contains the expected PR identifier fields consumed by the review loop.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: Two-pass result: Integration is minimum because failure appears at module contract boundary between writer hook and reader/enforcer hook via shared state format. Unit tests of either side alone can falsely pass while contract drifts. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond local file I/O; no AI dependency; no multi-agent sequence. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on compatibility/readability of persisted state format, which is reproducible with local temp files and in-process hooks.
behaviors.test_description: Execute post-tool hook logic to write review-gate state after simulated PR-create detection, then invoke pre-tool enforcement hook against that same state file and assert enforcement decision/path is activated as expected.
